title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: Most people are going to sleep at this time. We woke up early because we are heading to the Grand Canyon today. We have a very thrilling day ahead of us because we are going on a helicopter ride to the bottom of the Grand Canyon. So I'm a little bit nervous but very excited. But surprise, surprise we are running a little bit late to catch our bus. So let's hope we get there on time. We'll try our best. We can't miss this one this time. Okay thank you so much. We were late but somehow the bus is late too. So I think it's our lucky day. You excited for the Grand Canyon? Yeah.

The bus is packed. I was worried there weren't going to be any seats for us. I thought my uncle booked us a private tour but the first stop on the tour is breakfast. We're going to a restaurant to eat something before we head to the Grand Canyon. They gave us these cards to fill out and it's just the menu. I chose some scrambled eggs and some coffee because I think I need a boost of energy since we were up so early. You got to bring those into the restaurant.

So you should ride in the back with us. We're going to be making the comedian laugh. Tell him I got the health nut breakfast. It's scrambled eggs with veggies and cheese and some potatoes. I don't know if it's actually healthy but it sounded good. Of course I have to put a splash of hot sauce on it.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: Now we're on our way back to the bus and we will be heading to the Grand Canyon. I'm so excited although now the helicopter ride is getting a bit more real. I'm getting more nervous as it gets closer. Oh kimchi in the middle of the desert even definitely I'm shocked.

examples: Most people are going to sleep at this time. We woke up early because we are heading to the Grand Canyon today. title: have to / must
examples: So you should ride in the back with us. We're going to be making the comedian laugh. Tell him I got the health nut breakfast. It's scrambled eggs with veggies and cheese and some potatoes. I don't know if it's actually healthy but it sounded good. Of course I have to put a splash of hot sauce on it.

title: had better / it's time
examples: It's time for the helicopter ride. Ready or not? Here we go.

title: Passive (is done / was done)
examples: The Hoover Dam was built in the 1930s and at the time it was the tallest dam in the world.
